title: Authors and Citation
text: Kobosz (2026). stochgen: Simulation Stochastic Processes. R package version 0.1.0, https://antonikobosz.github.io/stochgen/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {stochgen: Simulation of Stochastic Processes},   author = {Antoni Kobosz},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://antonikobosz.github.io/stochgen/}, }

title: Simulation of Stochastic Processes
text: Simulation stochastic processes R. package provides lightweight tools simulating common stochastic processes : Brownian motion, Geometric Brownian Motion, Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process, Poisson jump processes. Core computations implemented C GSL OpenMP performance. information, visit: https://antonikobosz.github.io/stochgen/

title: Simulation of Stochastic Processes
code: install.packages("remotes")  remotes::install_github("AntoniKobosz/stochgen")

what: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
title: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
text: Simulates n_traj independent brownian motion paths following SDE: $$dS_t = \mu dt + \sigma dW_t$$ solution $$S_t = \mu t + \sigma W_t$$ \(W_t\) Wiener process. path, returns terminal value whether exceeded provided thresholds point.

title: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
code: brownian_end_info(   dt,   n_steps,   n_traj,   x0 = 0,   mu = 0,   sigma = 1,   lower_thresh = -1,   upper_thresh = 1 )

title: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
text: dt Time step size n_steps Number steps path n_traj Number simulated paths x0 Initial value mu Drift coefficient sigma Volatility coefficient lower_thresh Lower threshold. path flagged falls value point upper_thresh Upper threshold. path flagged exceeds value point

title: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
text: list three elements: xT numeric vector length n_paths containing terminal value simulated path. crossed_upper logical vector length n_paths. TRUE path exceeded upper threshold point. crossed_lower logical vector length n_paths. TRUE path fell lower threshold point.

title: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
text: Wiener process

title: Calculate final values of a bundle of brownian paths — brownian_end_info
code: # Simulate 100 paths of standard Brownian motion (mu=0, sigma=1) # over 100 steps of size 0.01 (total time T = 1) result <- brownian_end_info(dt = 0.01, n_steps = 100, n_traj = 100)  # Terminal values hist(result$xT, main = "Distribution of S_T", xlab = "S_T")   # Fraction of paths that crossed each threshold mean(result$crossed_upper) #> [1] 0.3 mean(result$crossed_lower) #> [1] 0.31

what: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
title: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
text: Simulates n_traj independent brownian motion paths following SDE: $$dS_t = \mu dt + \sigma dW_t$$ solution $$S_t = \mu t + \sigma W_t$$ \(W_t\) Wiener process.

title: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
code: brownian_matrix(dt, n_steps, n_traj, x0 = 0, mu = 0, sigma = 1)

title: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
text: dt Time step size n_steps Number steps path n_traj Number simulated paths x0 Initial value mu Drift coefficient sigma Volatility coefficient

title: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
text: Numeric matrix dimension n_steps x n_traj containing simulated paths. column corresponds one trajectory.

title: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
text: Wiener process

title: Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths — brownian_matrix
code: # Simulate a bundle of Brownian Motion paths M <- brownian_matrix(dt = 0.01, n_steps = 100, n_traj = 10) matplot(M, type = "l", lty = 1,         xlab = "step", ylab="S_t",         main = "Brownian Motion")

what: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
title: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
text: Simulates n_traj independent ornstein-uhlenbeck processes following SDE: $$dS_t = -\theta(S_t-\mu)dt + \sigma dW_t$$ \(W_t\) Wiener process. path, returns terminal value whether exceeded provided thresholds point.

title: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
code: ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info(   dt,   n_steps,   n_traj,   x0 = 0,   mu = 0,   theta = 1,   sigma = 1,   lower_thresh = -1,   upper_thresh = 1 )

title: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
text: dt Time step size n_steps Number steps path n_traj Number simulated paths x0 Initial value mu Drift coefficient theta Reversion rate sigma Volatility coefficient lower_thresh Lower threshold. path flagged falls value point upper_thresh Upper threshold. path flagged exceeds value point

title: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
text: list three elements: xT numeric vector length n_paths containing terminal value simulated path. crossed_upper logical vector length n_paths. TRUE path exceeded upper threshold point. crossed_lower logical vector length n_paths. TRUE path fell lower threshold point.

title: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
text: Ornstein–Uhlenbeck process

title: Calculate final values of a bundle ornstein-uhlenbeck processes — ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info
code: # Simulate 100 paths of Ornstein-Uhlenbeck process (mu=0, theta=1, sigma=1) # over 100 steps of size 0.01 (total time T = 1) result <- ornstein_uhlenbeck_end_info(dt = 0.01, n_steps = 100, n_traj = 100)  # Terminal values hist(result$xT, main = "Distribution of S_T", xlab = "S_T")   # Fraction of paths that crossed each threshold mean(result$crossed_upper) #> [1] 0.11 mean(result$crossed_lower) #> [1] 0.21

what: Generete a vector of jump times of a Poisson process — poiss_jump_times
title: Generete a vector of jump times of a Poisson process — poiss_jump_times
text: Simulates Poisson process intensity \(\lambda\) generating jump times \(t_1, t_2, \ldots, t_n\) first n jumps. Inter-arrival times follow Exponential distribution parameter \(\lambda\).

title: Generete a vector of jump times of a Poisson process — poiss_jump_times
code: poiss_jump_times(lambda, n)

title: Generete a vector of jump times of a Poisson process — poiss_jump_times
text: lambda intensity n number jumps

title: Generete a vector of jump times of a Poisson process — poiss_jump_times
text: Poisson point process

title: Generete a vector of jump times of a Poisson process — poiss_jump_times
code: # Simulate a Poisson process lambda <- 2 n <- 50 times <- poiss_jump_times(lambda, n)  t_max <- max(times) + 1/lambda # extra padding steps <- c(0, times, t_max) N <- 0:length(times)  # process value  plot(steps, c(N, tail(N, 1)),      type = "s",  # step function      xlab = "t", ylab = "N(t)",      main = expression("Poisson process, " ~ lambda == 2)) abline(a = 0, b = lambda, col = "red", lty = 2) legend("topleft", legend = expression(lambda * t), col = "red", lty = 2)
